target: lab-combined (db + web from same lab session)

findings.title: Connection and basic auth successful (dry-run synthetic)
findings.description: In a real run this would confirm supported auth mechanisms and detect weak/default accounts via safe probes.
findings.remediation: Ensure strong passwords, disable trust authentication, and prefer SCRAM/IDENT over cleartext where possible.

findings.title: Dangerous extension 'pg_read_server_files' present and grantable
findings.description: The extension allows reading server files via SQL; if granted to non-superuser roles this is a high-risk vector.
findings.evidence: dry-run: would have queried pg_available_extensions and pg_extension
findings.remediation: Revoke from non-superusers; only install extensions required by the workload.

findings.title: Excessive privileges detected on application role (dry-run)
findings.description: Role had broad grants (e.g. pg_read_all_data or FILE) not required for workload.
findings.evidence: dry-run: would have sampled postgres privilege views with row limit
findings.remediation: Apply principle of least privilege; use read-only roles for reporting users.

findings.title: Version fingerprint (dry-run synthetic)
findings.description: Version string would be obtained via safe method (e.g. version() or @@version).
findings.remediation: Keep DB engine patched; cross-reference disclosed CVEs against your exposure.

findings.title: Advanced test vector (dry-run simulation)
findings.description: Would attempt a safe, bounded, reversible file-read or command vector in an isolated lab DB (e.g. via a temporary UDF or extension that is immediately dropped).
findings.evidence: dry-run only; no connection or mutation performed
findings.remediation: Review the exact vector, run only on throwaway lab instances, ensure auto-cleanup, and revoke privileges after the test.

findings.title: DB pentest execution metadata
findings.description: db_type=postgres scan_type=db-pentest queries_executed=42 dry_run=true manifest_matched=false duration_ms=0
findings.evidence: dry-run: synthesizing representative findings for all requested checks; advanced: dry-run simulation of reversible test vectors requested; advanced: would execute 'file-read-via-extension' (reversible: CREATE FUNCTION ...; SELECT ...; DROP FUNCTION) then auto-clean; advanced: cleanup hook stub invoked (no-op in pure dry-run; real path would DROP created objects); dry-run: no database connection attempted; dry-run: would execute up to 100 queries within 120s budget

findings.title: Error-based SQLi in search parameter (synthetic from web fuzzer)
findings.description: Database error message disclosed SQL syntax during fuzzing of the lab web app fronting the same DB.
findings.recommendation: Use parameterized queries and strict input validation.
